questions.question: Telecom Regulatory Authority of India (TRAI) was established in the year _______.
questions.explanation: The Telecom Regulatory Authority of India (TRAI) was established with effect from 20th February 1997 by an Act of Parliament, called the Telecom Regulatory Authority of India Act, 1997, to regulate telecom services, including fixation/revision of tariffs for telecom services which were earlier vested in the Central Government.

questions.question: ISRO is headquartered in which city?
questions.explanation: The Indian Space Research Organization is the space agency of the Government of India headquartered in the city of Bengaluru.

questions.question: UGC is a statutory body set up by the Indian Union government. What does "U" stand for?
questions.explanation: The University Grants Commission of India (UGC India) is a statutory body set up by the Indian Union government in accordance to the UGC Act 1956 under Ministry of Human Resource Development, and is charged with coordination, determination and maintenance of standards of higher education.

questions.question: NSSO is mandated to perform __________.
questions.explanation: The National Sample Survey Office (NSSO) headed by a Director General is responsible for conduct of large scale sample surveys in diverse fields on All India basis.

questions.question: Archaeological Survey of India comes under which Ministry?
questions.explanation: The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI), under the Ministry of Culture, is the premier organization for the archaeological researches and protection of the cultural heritage of the nation.

questions.question: Confederation of Indian Industry was established in _______.
questions.explanation: The Confederation of Indian Industry (CII) works to create and sustain an environment conducive to the development of India, partnering industry, Government, and civil society, through advisory and consultative processes. It was established in 1895.

questions.question: FSSAI is related to ___________
questions.explanation: FSSAI stands for Food Safety and Standards Authority of India. Food Safety and Standards Authority of India is an autonomous body established under the Ministry of Health & Family Welfare, Government of India.

questions.question: Bureau of Indian Standards is headquartered in ___________
questions.explanation: BIS has its Headquarters at New Delhi. BIS is the National Standard Body of India established under the BIS Act 1986 for the harmonious development of the activities of standardization, marking and quality certification of goods.

questions.question: IRDAI is associated with _________.
questions.explanation: IRDAI stands for Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority of India. It is an autonomous, statutory agency tasked with regulating and promoting the insurance and re-insurance industries in India.

questions.question: What "I" stands for in CSIR?
questions.explanation: Council of Scientific & Industrial Research (CSIR) is a premier national R&D organisation, is among the world's largest publicly funded R&D organisation. It was established by the Government of India in 1942 is an autonomous body.

questions.question: CBFC is associated with which industry?
questions.explanation: Central Board of Film Certification is a statutory censorship and classification body under the Ministry of Information and Broadcasting, Government of India.

questions.question: FICCI is associated with _________.
questions.explanation: A non-government, not-for-profit organisation, FICCI is the voice of India's business and industry. Established in 1927, FICCI is the largest and oldest apex business organisation in India. Its history is closely interwoven with India's struggle for independence, its industrialization, and its emergence as one of the most rapidly growing global economies.

questions.question: DRDO comes under which ministry?
questions.explanation: Defence Research & Development Organisation (DRDO) works under Department of Defence Research and Development of Ministry of Defence.

questions.question: All India Radio was founded in ________.
questions.explanation: All India Radio (AIR), officially known since 1956 as Akashvani, is the national public radio broadcaster of India and a division of Prasar Bharati. It was established in 1930.

questions.question: What does "S" stand for in CSO?
questions.explanation: CSO stands for Central Statistics Office. The Central Statistics Office coordinates the statistical activities in the country and evolves statistical standards. It is headed by a Director General assisted by 5 Additional Director Generals.

questions.question: RBI was established in the year ________.
questions.explanation: The Reserve Bank of India was established on April 1, 1935. The Central Office of the Reserve Bank was initially established in Calcutta but was permanently moved to Mumbai in 1937.

questions.question: What does "C" stand for in ICSI?
questions.explanation: ICSI stands for Institute of Company Secretaries of India. It is a statutory professional body in India with the objective of promoting, regulating and developing the profession of Company Secretaries in India.

questions.question: NASSCOM was established in the year __________?
questions.explanation: The National Association of Software and Services Companies is a trade association of Indian Information Technology and Business Process Outsourcing industry. Established in 1988, NASSCOM is a non-profit organisation.

questions.question: Who was the founder director of CBI?
questions.explanation: The Central Bureau of Investigation traces its origin to the Special Police Establishment (SPE) which was set up in 1941 by the Government of India. The founder director of the CBI was Shri D.P. Kohli who held office from 1st April, 1963 to 31st May, 1968.

questions.question: Securities and Exchange Board of India was established in the year ______?
questions.explanation: The Securities and Exchange Board of India was established on April 12, 1992 in accordance with the provisions of the Securities and Exchange Board of India Act, 1992.

questions.question: Which among the following is the primary foreign intelligence agency of India?
questions.explanation: The Research and Analysis Wing (RAW) is the primary foreign intelligence agency of India. It was established in 1968.

questions.question: Which among the following is India's premier nuclear research facility?
questions.explanation: A premier multi-disciplinary Nuclear Research Centre of India having excellent infrastructure for advanced Research and Development with expertise covering the entire spectrum of Nuclear Science and Engineering and related areas.

questions.question: SSC is attached to which department?
options: Department of Scientific and Industrial Research
questions.explanation: The Staff Selection Commission (SSC) is an attached office of the Department of Personnel and Training and comprises of Chairman, two Members and a Secretary-cum-Controller of Examinations who are appointed on such terms and conditions as may be prescribed by the Central Government from time to time.

questions.question: The Competition Commission of India was established in the year ________?
questions.explanation: The Competition Commission of India (CCI) was established by the Central Government with effect from 14th October 2003. CCI consists of a Chairperson and 6 Members appointed by the Central Government. It is the duty of the Commission to eliminate practices having adverse effect on competition, promote and sustain competition, protect the interests of consumers and ensure freedom of trade in the markets of India.

questions.question: BSNL is headquartered at _________?
questions.explanation: Bharat Sanchar Nigam Limited is an Indian state-owned telecommunications company headquartered in New Delhi.

questions.question: NABARD is headquartered at ________?
questions.explanation: National Bank for Agriculture and Rural Development (NABARD) is an apex development financial institution in India, headquartered at Mumbai. It is aimed at building an empowered and financially inclusive rural India.

questions.question: Who among the following is the chairperson of GST Council?
options: Member elected by state finance ministers
questions.explanation: As per Article 279A of the amended Constitution, the GST Council which will be a joint forum of the Centre and the States, shall consist of the following members: - a) Union Finance Minister - Chairperson b) The Union Minister of State, in-charge of Revenue of finance - Member c) The Minister In-charge of finance or taxation or any other Minister nominated by each State Government - Members.

questions.question: Indian Banks Association was formed in the year _________?
questions.explanation: Indian Banks' Association, formed on 26 September 1946 as a representative body of management of banking in India operating in India - an association of Indian banks and financial institutions based in Mumbai.

questions.question: What does "P" stand for in NPCI?
questions.explanation: National Payments Corporation of India (NPCI) is an umbrella organization for all retail payments in India. It was set up with the guidance and support of the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) and Indian Banks Association (IBA).

questions.question: Unique Identification Authority of India comes under which ministry?
options: Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation
Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology
questions.explanation: The Unique Identification Authority of India (UIDAI) is a statutory authority established under the provisions of the Aadhaar Act, 2016 on 12 July 2016 by the Government of India, under the Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology.
